summary_one_line: Winners produce input bytes that materialize a Curl writer_stack before cleanup.

hypotheses.what_input_feature: Input must trigger Curl_unencode_write path so writer_stack is non-NULL at cleanup time
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: Winner inputs successfully parse a Content-Encoding header causing writer chain to be built
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: Loser inputs fail upstream parsing so writer_stack is never populated
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: I2SRandReplace substitutes literal byte sequences from CMP operands so the Content-Encoding parser path is reached

evidence_trail.claim: The blocker is at content_encoding.c:1016 while(writer)
Losers reach line 1016 but never enter the loop body

mechanism_consistency_check.verification_method: Ran `python3 tools/db_query.py lineage --branch 19 --role W --fuzzer cmplog --trial 1 --seed 006459fd40731a4e`; chain has 21 ancestor levels; depths 19 and 20 are I2S-floor rows (mutation_op = -, ParentInfo-only metadata). Per fuzzer_mechanism_library.md cmplog section, dash rows are exclusive to cmplog/vpc under the current LibAFL build and indicate an unwrapped I2SRandReplace find (lower bound — some I2S finds leak into the havoc bucket).

falsifiability.would_be_refuted_by: If a hand-crafted input that takes the F branch at line 1016 by setting writer_stack via a different code path (e.g., not via Content-Encoding) yields cleanup with writer=NULL, the writer-stack hypothesis is incomplete.

weakest_evidence_point: Floor signal is a lower bound — the cited dash rows confirm I2S contributed somewhere in the ancestry, but the immediate leaf mutation was havoc, so we cannot prove the *byte that unlocked* the gate came from I2S vs from later havoc on an I2S-seeded ancestor.
